name: Paul Motian
description: American jazz drummer, percussionist, and composer (1931-2011)
abstract: Stephen Paul Motian (March 25, 1931 – November 22, 2011) was an American jazz drummer, percussionist, and composer of Armenian descent. He played an important role in freeing jazz drummers from strict time-keeping duties.

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: the Bill Evans homage that you did with Eddie Gomez, your old friend, and Paul Motian... So during these two weeks here at the Blue Note, it was a float for me to experience what happened every set.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: He put together a trio because Paul Motian... and the bass player was Teddy Kottick. And we went in and did a basically trio record... Paul and Scott and Bill
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: David Friesen
about_quotes.evidence: Paul Motian on that gig, I didn’t even give him music. He says, “Where’s my music?” I says, “I don’t give drummers music, just listen, Paul.”
about_quotes.doc_title: David Friesen interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 24, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: the record I made at the Half Note with Warne, Paul Motian, Jimmy Garrison, and Bill Evans
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Israels
about_quotes.evidence: The first drummer was Paul Motian... I was glad to be in the band... Then Paul left
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Israels interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: he always stood next to me because I had an amplifier and microphones … and he wanted to hear the bass so he stood next to me most of the time, or Paul, or whoever the bass player was, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
